title: Turkey’s Drone Industry at a Strategic Crossroads
author: Doga Eralp
summary:
Following years of quiet ascent, Turkey’s combat drone industry now finds itself at a moment of decision. Having secured a rapidly expanding footprint across Africa, Central Asia, and the Middle East, Ankara is now pivoting toward NATO and European defense-industrial integration. But this dual-track strategy — expanding defense exports to fragile and conflict-affected regions and embedding in the NATO ecosystem — may no longer be sustainable as geopolitical polarization intensifies.In this new phase, Turkish drone diplomacy is forced to confront a basic but difficult choice: pursue global market dominance with few constraints, or integrate into Western military-industrial structures that demand
